articles.headline: Big Tech’s AI Spending Race Heads Toward $600B
articles.summary: Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and other tech giants are projected to spend $600 billion on AI infrastructure including chips, data centers, and cloud capacity in 2026, shifting AI from software to a capital-intensive buildout. This elevates compute access as a strategic advantage, altering economics for startups, cloud providers, and chipmakers while raising investor scrutiny on returns amid earnings season.[1]

articles.headline: 5 Business Technology Trends Leaders Can't Ignore in 2026
articles.summary: Agentic AI is advancing from pilots to production, enabling SMEs to redesign processes around AI agents for consistent execution, growth without added headcount, and reduced management layers. Physical AI via robotics-as-a-service lowers barriers for small and mid-sized businesses to automate physical tasks, exemplified by IoT projects cutting service visits by 30% while generating new revenue.[3]

articles.headline: OpenAI’s $500 Billion Stargate Data Center Project Undergoes Major Restructuring
articles.summary: OpenAI restructured its massive Stargate project with flexible partnerships to address rising costs and maintain AI leadership. This signals ongoing investment opportunities in data centers, energy, and chips but introduces execution risks and supply-chain pressures for infrastructure providers.
